---
title: Paths
author: Pierre Casadebaig
date: '2021-09-14'
splash:
  image: ./splash/background.svg
  caption: Paths
  suffix: 'Pierre Casadebaig'
trailer: ""
---

<style>
.splash-caption-tweak {color: white; text-shadow: 0px 0px 15px black}
.splash-caption-suffix {color: white; text-shadow: 0px 0px 15px black}
</style>

> A visual blog about algorithmic art, mainly designed for pen plotters.

I am using the [R](https://www.r-project.org/) programming language to write algorithms that generate digital outputs. Rather than printing, I am interested in using a pen-plotter to draw them on paper. While this process enables drawing with enhanced repetition and precision, it limits the use of fills or line width variation.

This site presents some digital and real outputs and documents the ideas underlying the functioning of the algorithms. Most of the code to study and reproduce these outputs is released [here](https://github.com/picasa/generative) with an open license. Overall, making visual art is very new to me, in my day job I am a scientist working in plant biology. Feel free to contact me via [email](https://github.com/picasa) or [Twitter](https://twitter.com/pircasa) for any questions related to this blog.
